name: irobot_create_msgs
description: Package containing action, message, and service definitions used by the iRobot(R) Create(R) platform

AudioNote.description: Play a note (frequency) for a specific duration
AudioNote.raw:
# Play a note (frequency) for a specific duration
uint16 frequency    # Frequency of note in Hz.
builtin_interfaces/Duration max_runtime # Time note should play for.

fields.name: frequency
fields.comment: Frequency of note in Hz.

fields.name: max_runtime
fields.comment: Time note should play for.

AudioNoteVector.description: Play a sequence of audio notes.
AudioNoteVector.raw:
# Play a sequence of audio notes.
std_msgs/Header header
irobot_create_msgs/AudioNote[] notes
# If the notes should be appended or overwrite the currently playing notes.
bool append

Button.description: This message gives the status for a button
Button.raw:
# This message gives the status for a button

std_msgs/Header header                            # Header stamp is time and frame at which information was collected
bool is_pressed                                   # Whether or not button is depressed
builtin_interfaces/Time last_start_pressed_time   # The last time a button press was initiated
builtin_interfaces/Duration last_pressed_duration # The duration that the button was depressed

fields.name: header
fields.comment: Header stamp is time and frame at which information was collected

fields.name: is_pressed
fields.comment: Whether or not button is depressed

fields.name: last_start_pressed_time
fields.comment: The last time a button press was initiated

fields.name: last_pressed_duration
fields.comment: The duration that the button was depressed

DockStatus.description: This message contains information about whether the robot is docked or sees the dock in its sensors.
DockStatus.raw:
# This message contains information about whether the robot is docked or sees the dock in its sensors.

std_msgs/Header header  # Header stamp is when dock info was queried.
bool dock_visible       # Whether robot sees dock in its sensors used for docking
bool is_docked          # Whether the robot is docked

fields.name: header
fields.comment: Header stamp is when dock info was queried.

fields.name: dock_visible
fields.comment: Whether robot sees dock in its sensors used for docking

fields.name: is_docked
fields.comment: Whether the robot is docked

HazardDetection.description: This message describes a detected hazard.
HazardDetection.raw:
# This message describes a detected hazard.
# The frame ID allows to retrieve the location of the sensor that made the detection.

# The robot has reached its backup limit. It will not drive further backward for safety reasons.
# You can disable this limit through the Create3 "safety_override" parameter by setting it 
# to "backup_only" or "full".
# The Create3 webserver can be used to set a default value for the parameter.
uint8 BACKUP_LIMIT=0
# The robot has bumped against an obstacle
uint8 BUMP=1
# The robot detected a cliff
uint8 CLIFF=2
# The wheels of the robot are stalled against an obstacle
uint8 STALL=3
# The wheels of the robot are fully dropped
uint8 WHEEL_DROP=4
# The robot detects an obstacle in close proximity
uint8 OBJECT_PROXIMITY=5

std_msgs/Header header
uint8 type

HazardDetectionVector.description: This message contains a vector of detected hazards.
HazardDetectionVector.raw:
# This message contains a vector of detected hazards.

std_msgs/Header header
irobot_create_msgs/HazardDetection[] detections

InterfaceButtons.description: This message is the status of the 3 interface buttons on the Create faceplate
InterfaceButtons.raw:
# This message is the status of the 3 interface buttons on the Create faceplate
std_msgs/Header header # Header stamp is time at which information was collected
irobot_create_msgs/Button button_1     # Left button on faceplate marked with 1 dot
irobot_create_msgs/Button button_power # Center Power button on faceplate
irobot_create_msgs/Button button_2     # Right button on faceplate marked with 2 dots

fields.name: header
fields.comment: Header stamp is time at which information was collected

fields.name: button_1
fields.comment: Left button on faceplate marked with 1 dot

fields.name: button_power
fields.comment: Center Power button on faceplate

fields.name: button_2
fields.comment: Right button on faceplate marked with 2 dots

IrIntensity.description: This message provides the ir intensity readings
IrIntensity.raw:
# This message provides the ir intensity readings

std_msgs/Header header
int16 value

IrIntensityVector.description: This message provides the ir intensity readings
IrIntensityVector.raw:
# This message provides the ir intensity readings

std_msgs/Header header
irobot_create_msgs/IrIntensity[] readings

IrOpcode.description: This message describes a detected ir opcode.
IrOpcode.raw:
# This message describes a detected ir opcode.

uint8 CODE_IR_FORCE_FIELD=161
uint8 CODE_IR_VIRTUAL_WALL=162
uint8 CODE_IR_BUOY_GREEN=164
uint8 CODE_IR_BUOY_RED=168
uint8 CODE_IR_BUOY_BOTH=172
uint8 CODE_IR_EVAC_GREEN_FIELD=244
uint8 CODE_IR_EVAC_RED_FIELD=248
uint8 CODE_IR_EVAC_BOTH_FIELD=252

uint8 SENSOR_OMNI=0
uint8 SENSOR_DIRECTIONAL_FRONT=1

std_msgs/Header header      # time when we saw the opcode
uint8 opcode                # opcode
uint8 sensor                # the sensor which saw the opcode

fields.name: header
fields.comment: time when we saw the opcode

fields.name: opcode
fields.comment: opcode

fields.name: sensor
fields.comment: the sensor which saw the opcode

KidnapStatus.description: This message provides the robot kidnap status
KidnapStatus.raw:
# This message provides the robot kidnap status

std_msgs/Header header
bool is_kidnapped

LedColor.description: This message is to set RGB values (0-255) for an LED
LedColor.raw:
# This message is to set RGB values (0-255) for an LED
uint8 red
uint8 green
uint8 blue

LightringLeds.description: This message sets the RGB values of the lightright LEDs
LightringLeds.raw:
# This message sets the RGB values of the lightright LEDs
std_msgs/Header header
# If you look at the lightring as an analog clock with 12 at the front
# of the robot
# led index 0 is 4 o'clock,
# led index 1 is 2 o'clock,
# led index 2 is 12 o'clock,
# led index 3 is 10 o'clock,
# led index 4 is 8 o'clock,
# led index 5 is 6 o'clock
irobot_create_msgs/LedColor[6] leds
# Whether to override system's default light patterns with given command
# Set to false to return control of lights to system
bool override_system

Mouse.description: This message contains a mouse raw measurement.
Mouse.raw:
# This message contains a mouse raw measurement.

std_msgs/Header header    # Header stamp should be acquisition time of mouse measure.
float32 integrated_x      # Motions in the X dimension in meters.
float32 integrated_y      # Motions in the Y dimension in meters.
uint32 frame_id           # Value incremented every time mouse stops tracking
uint8 last_squal          # Surface quality level. It's proportional to the number of valid features visible by the sensor.

fields.name: header
fields.comment: Header stamp should be acquisition time of mouse measure.

fields.name: integrated_x
fields.comment: Motions in the X dimension in meters.

fields.name: integrated_y
fields.comment: Motions in the Y dimension in meters.

fields.name: frame_id
fields.comment: Value incremented every time mouse stops tracking

fields.name: last_squal
fields.comment: Surface quality level. It's proportional to the number of valid features visible by the sensor.

SlipStatus.description: This message provides the robot slip status
SlipStatus.raw:
# This message provides the robot slip status

std_msgs/Header header
bool is_slipping

StopStatus.description: This message provides the robot stopped status
StopStatus.raw:
# This message provides the robot stopped status

std_msgs/Header header
bool is_stopped

WheelStatus.description: This message contains status on the wheels
WheelStatus.raw:
# This message contains status on the wheels

std_msgs/Header header              # Header stamp should be acquisition time of measure.
int16 current_ma_left               # Current measurement for left wheel in milliamps 
int16 current_ma_right              # Current measurement for right wheel in milliamps
int16 pwm_left                      # PWM % duty cycle measurement (where int16::max is +100%) for left wheel 
int16 pwm_right                     # PWM % duty cycle measurement (where int16::max is +100%) for right wheel
bool wheels_enabled                 # Whether wheels are enabled or disabled (disabled when E-Stopped)

fields.name: header
fields.comment: Header stamp should be acquisition time of measure.

fields.name: current_ma_left
fields.comment: Current measurement for left wheel in milliamps

fields.name: current_ma_right
fields.comment: Current measurement for right wheel in milliamps

fields.name: pwm_left
fields.comment: PWM % duty cycle measurement (where int16::max is +100%) for left wheel

fields.name: pwm_right
fields.comment: PWM % duty cycle measurement (where int16::max is +100%) for right wheel

fields.name: wheels_enabled
fields.comment: Whether wheels are enabled or disabled (disabled when E-Stopped)

WheelTicks.description: This message contains wheel ticks raw measurements.
WheelTicks.raw:
# This message contains wheel ticks raw measurements.
# It can be used both for publishing integrated as well as absolute encoder readings.

std_msgs/Header header      # Header stamp should be acquisition time of measure.
int32 ticks_left            # Count of encoder ticks for left wheel.
int32 ticks_right           # Count of encoder ticks for right wheel.

fields.name: header
fields.comment: Header stamp should be acquisition time of measure.

fields.name: ticks_left
fields.comment: Count of encoder ticks for left wheel.

fields.name: ticks_right
fields.comment: Count of encoder ticks for right wheel.

WheelVels.description: This message contains wheel velocity measurements.
WheelVels.raw:
# This message contains wheel velocity measurements.

std_msgs/Header header              # Header stamp should be acquisition time of measure.
float32 velocity_left               # Velocity measure for left wheel in rad/sec.
float32 velocity_right              # Velocity measure for right wheel in rad/sec.

fields.name: header
fields.comment: Header stamp should be acquisition time of measure.

fields.name: velocity_left
fields.comment: Velocity measure for left wheel in rad/sec.

fields.name: velocity_right
fields.comment: Velocity measure for right wheel in rad/sec.

EStop.raw:
bool e_stop_on # true enables e-stop disabling drive motor power, false disables e-stop re-enabling motor power
---
bool success   # indicate successful application of command
string message # informational for result

request_fields.name: e_stop_on
request_fields.comment: true enables e-stop disabling drive motor power, false disables e-stop re-enabling motor power

ResetPose.description: This service reset the robot pose estimate to the provided one
ResetPose.raw:
# This service reset the robot pose estimate to the provided one

geometry_msgs/Pose pose
---

RobotPower.description: This service cuts power from the battery to the system, powering off robot and all accessories
RobotPower.raw:
# This service cuts power from the battery to the system, powering off robot and all accessories

---
bool success   # indicate successful application of command, robot may power down before response is transmitted
string message # informational for result, robot may power down before response is transmitted

response_fields.name: success
response_fields.comment: indicate successful application of command, robot may power down before response is transmitted

response_fields.name: message
response_fields.comment: informational for result, robot may power down before response is transmitted

AudioNoteSequence.raw:
# Request
# Special number of iterations to signify play sequence until canceled
int32 INFINITE = -1

# Number of note_sequence iterations to play, defaults to 1
int32 iterations 1
# Sequence of notes to play per iteration
irobot_create_msgs/AudioNoteVector note_sequence
---
# Result
# Whether sequence played all requested iterations
bool complete
# Number of iterations of note sequence that were played
int32 iterations_played
# Runtime of audio playback
builtin_interfaces/Duration runtime
---
# Feedback
# Number of iterations of note sequence that have been played
int32 iterations_played
# Runtime of audio playback so far
builtin_interfaces/Duration current_runtime

Dock.raw:
# Request
---
# Result
bool is_docked
---
# Feedback
bool sees_dock

DriveArc.raw:
# Request
int8 TRANSLATE_FORWARD = 1
int8 TRANSLATE_BACKWARD = -1

# Whether to arc forward or backward from robot's current position
int8 translate_direction
# Relative angle (radians) for robot to rotate along arc from current heading.  Angles greater than 2 PI will cause the robot to rotate in multiple circles
float32 angle
# Radius of arc (meters) for robot to drive along
float32 radius
# Max translation speed (positive m/s), will cap negative distance to negative speed
float32 max_translation_speed 0.3
---
# Result
# Pose where robot finished
geometry_msgs/PoseStamped pose
---
# Feedback
# Remaining radians to rotate
float32 remaining_angle_travel

DriveDistance.raw:
# Request

# Drive a distance (meters) in a straight line
float32 distance
# Max translation speed (positive m/s), will cap negative distance to negative speed
float32 max_translation_speed 0.3
---
# Result
# Pose where robot finished
geometry_msgs/PoseStamped pose
---
# Feedback
# How much distance is left to travel
float32 remaining_travel_distance

LedAnimation.raw:
# Request
# Supported Animation types
int8 BLINK_LIGHTS = 1
int8 SPIN_LIGHTS = 2

# Animation to apply
int8 animation_type
# LED values to apply to animation
irobot_create_msgs/LightringLeds lightring
# Time to apply animation
builtin_interfaces/Duration max_runtime
---
# Result
builtin_interfaces/Duration runtime
---
# Feedback
# Time the animation has left to run
builtin_interfaces/Duration remaining_runtime

NavigateToPosition.raw:
# Request

# Drive to goal position in odometry frame using simple rotate/translate/rotate approach
geometry_msgs/PoseStamped goal_pose
# Whether to achieve goal heading for final orientation or just use position
bool achieve_goal_heading
# Max translation speed (positive m/s), will cap negative distance to negative speed
float32 max_translation_speed 0.3
# Max rotation speed (positive rad/s), will cap negative angle to negative speed
float32 max_rotation_speed 1.9
---
# Result
# Pose where robot finished
geometry_msgs/PoseStamped pose
---
# Feedback
# Whether robot is in first phase rotating to face travel direction to goal position
int8 ROTATING_TO_GOAL_POSITION = 1
# Whether robot is in second phase translating to to goal position
int8 DRIVING_TO_GOAL_POSITION = 2
# Whether robot is in third phase rotating to face goal orientation
int8 ROTATING_TO_GOAL_ORIENTATION = 3
# Which of the 3 phases above that robot is currently in
int8 navigate_state
# Remaining radians to rotate when robot is ROTATING_TO_GOAL_POSITION or ROTATING_TO_GOAL_ORIENTATION
float32 remaining_angle_travel
# How much distance is left to travel when robot is DRIVING_TO_GOAL_POSITION
float32 remaining_travel_distance

RotateAngle.raw:
# Request

# Rotate for relative angle (radians) from current robot position.  Angles greater than 2 PI will cause the robot to rotate in multiple circles
float32 angle
# Max rotation speed (positive rad/s), will cap negative angle to negative speed
float32 max_rotation_speed 1.9
---
# Result
# Pose where robot finished
geometry_msgs/PoseStamped pose
---
# Feedback
# Remaining radians to rotate
float32 remaining_angle_travel

WallFollow.raw:
# Request
int8 FOLLOW_RIGHT = -1
int8 FOLLOW_LEFT = 1

int8 follow_side
builtin_interfaces/Duration max_runtime
---
# Result
builtin_interfaces/Duration runtime
---
# Feedback
bool engaged
